response.justification: Candidate has strong general software engineering experience across web, backend, and some embedded-adjacent work, but lacks the core requirements for this role: native-level Japanese proficiency is not indicated (only English, Romanian, and basic German), and there is minimal evidence of C programming for UNIX in recent roles, Linux kernel modification, embedded networking, or security technologies like MACsec, 802.1X, or switch firmware. The Japanese language requirement alone is a hard disqualifier.
strengths: Long, diverse software engineering career with exposure to C/C++ at National Instruments and low-level systems work in DLang
Strong testing discipline (TDD, CI/CD, workshops on testing) which aligns with embedded QA collaboration needs
Linux familiarity and some hardware-adjacent experience (robotic arms, wave field synthesis with Jack audio)
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency listed — hard requirement not met
No demonstrated experience with LAN-switch firmware, MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, NETCONF, SNMP, or chip-vendor SDKs
No evidence of Linux kernel source modification or embedded security work; recent career is web/cloud-focused (TypeScript, Node, AWS, MongoDB)

key_factors.factor: Native Japanese language requirement not met
Lack of embedded networking and switch security experience
Some C/C++ and Linux background from earlier roles
